summary: Ukrainian forces conducted a large‑scale drone strike on the Moscow region, hitting the Angstrem semiconductor plant, oil facilities and the Belbek airfield in Crimea. Russian‑linked missile and Shahed attacks continue across multiple fronts, with notable concentrations on Dnipro, Kherson and Zaporizhzhia. Air and missile threats, including possible Iskander‑M launches, keep the overall situation volatile.
key_points: Ukrainian drones struck Angstrem plant and oil stations in Moscow region.
Belbek airfield in Crimea damaged by Ukrainian special operations.
Over 3,170 attack drones, 1,300 KAB missiles and 74 rockets launched by Russia this week.
Shahed attacks reported on Dnipro, Kherson, Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions.
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on NE and SE axes.
Potential Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch warned from Krasnodar Krai.
Casualties: 3 Russians killed, at least 16 injured in Moscow‑region strike.

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces executed the war’s largest drone raid on the Moscow region, damaging the Angstrem semiconductor plant, multiple oil facilities and the Belbek airfield in Crimea, killing three Russians and injuring at least 16. Russia responded with a high volume of Shahed drones and KAB missiles across the Dnipro, Kherson and Zaporizhzhia axes, while warning of possible Iskander‑M launches from Krasnodar. The combined air‑ground activity keeps the frontlines highly volatile.

points: Ukrainian special operations, coordinated with the Alpha Center, hit the Angstrem semiconductor plant in the Moscow region, a key supplier for Russian defense electronics (Source: @UkraineNow/81280).
Drone strikes damaged two oil‑pumping stations – Sonyechnogorskaya and Volodarskoye – and the Moscow refinery, disrupting fuel logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81280).
Belbek airfield in occupied Crimea suffered infrastructure damage, reducing Russian air‑operational capacity in the Black Sea region (Source: @DeepStateUA/23512).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV groups targeting Dnipro from the north, east and south, indicating coordinated multi‑directional pressure (Source: @kpszsu/62141).
Ukrainian intelligence noted increased tactical aviation threats on the north‑east and south‑east fronts, suggesting preparation for deeper strikes (Source: @kpszsu/62133).

Russian forces launched over 3,170 attack drones, more than 1,300 KAB cruise missiles and 74 ballistic rockets across Ukraine in the past week, indicating sustained offensive pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/81277).
Shahed loitering munitions struck Dnipro (9 from Kherson, 3 from Zaporizhzhia), Kharkiv, Sumy and other oblasts, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
A Shahed impact in Enerhodar caused property damage but no injuries, showing the spread of attacks into the south (Source: @tass_agency/376280).
Russian tactical aviation activity intensified on the north‑east and south‑east axes, increasing the threat to frontline settlements (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Russian authorities warned of a possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from Krasnodar Krai, heightening strategic risk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66799).
Shahed attacks from Sumy targeted Chernihiv region (2 missiles) and from Kharkiv hit Dnipro district, indicating cross‑border UAV penetration (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Ukrainian UAV groups reported over the Donetsk area, with 11 drones from Donetsk heading toward Synelnykove, showing intra‑regional contest (Source: @eRadarrua/92322).
Russian tactical aviation activity noted on the north‑east direction, threatening front‑line towns in Kharkiv and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Multiple Shahed clusters reported in Zaporizhzhia (5) and Kherson (9) aimed at Dnipro, reflecting coordinated eastern‑southern pressure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Ukrainian UAVs observed heading from Poltava and Dnipropetrovsk toward the right bank of the Dnipro, suggesting attempts to interdict supply lines (Source: @eRadarrua/92335).

Ukrainian UAVs detected over Zhytomyr heading to Ovruch and over Chernihiv heading to Chernihiv, Slavutych and Pryluky, indicating reconnaissance and strike missions near the front (Source: @kpszsu/62141).
New UAV groups reported from the north of Kyiv region targeting Prypiat, showing pressure on the Chernobyl exclusion zone (Source: @kpszsu/62095).
Ukrainian drones observed over Sumy heading to Sumy and Chernihiv, maintaining pressure on Russian‑held positions (Source: @kpszsu/62143).
Russian tactical aviation activity on the north‑east axis increases risk to frontline settlements in Chernihiv and Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Kyiv region near Vyshhorod, suggesting potential targeting of logistics hubs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Shahed attacks on Dnipro from Kherson (9) and Zaporizhzhia (3) caused civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
One Shahed hit Kremenchuk from Dnipropetrovsk, extending the threat to central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66788).
Ukrainian UAV groups reported over Dnipro from the south‑west and south‑east, indicating attempts to contest river crossings (Source: @kpszsu/62126).
Russian missile and drone activity continued over Kherson and Zaporizhzhia, with multiple Shahed clusters reported (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity near the right bank of the Dnipro, targeting supply routes near Lykhovka and Pavlish (Source: @eRadarrua/92335).
Ukrainian special operations damaged the Belbek airfield in Crimea, reducing Russian air‑base capability (Source: @DeepStateUA/23512).
Russian tactical aviation increased on north‑east and south‑east fronts, raising the risk of air strikes on Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Potential Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch warned from Krasnodar Krai, indicating escalation of long‑range strike capability (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66799).
Ukrainian UAVs operating from multiple directions (north, east, south) suggest a broadened aerial reconnaissance and strike network (Source: @kpszsu/62141).
Russian air defenses reported engagement of multiple Shahed loitering munitions across Dnipro, Kherson and Zaporizhzhia, showing high air‑defence activity (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).

key_developments: Ukrainian drones struck the Angstrem semiconductor plant, two oil‑pumping stations and a refinery in the Moscow region, killing three Russians and injuring at least 16 (Source: @UkraineNow/81280).
Belbek airfield in occupied Crimea was damaged by Ukrainian special operations, degrading Russian air‑operational capacity (Source: @DeepStateUA/23512).
Russia launched over 3,170 attack drones, 1,300 KAB missiles and 74 rockets across Ukraine in the past week, maintaining high offensive pressure (Source: @UkraineNow/81277).
Shahed loitering munitions hit Dnipro (9 from Kherson, 3 from Zaporizhzhia) and other oblasts, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66791).
Russian tactical aviation activity increased on the north‑east and south‑east axes, threatening frontline settlements in Kharkiv, Sumy and the Donbas (Source: @kpszsu/62133).
Ukrainian UAV groups reported multiple inbound flights toward Dnipro, Prypiat and Kyiv regions, indicating coordinated multi‑directional operations (Source: @kpszsu/62141).
A possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch was warned from Krasnodar Krai, raising strategic escalation risk (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66799).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian strike capabilities have expanded to deep‑strike UAV operations against high‑value Russian industrial and military targets, while Russia continues to rely on massed drone and missile barrages to pressure Ukrainian rear areas. The emergence of potential Iskander‑M launches adds a strategic ballistic threat, and increased Russian tactical aviation heightens the risk of air strikes on front‑line positions. Both sides are intensifying aerial contestation, suggesting a protracted, high‑intensity conflict with elevated civilian risk.
sections.regional_response: Western intelligence outlets have highlighted the significance of the Moscow‑region strike, indicating possible escalation in Kyiv's offensive posture. Russian officials, however, have downplayed the impact while emphasizing continued missile and drone offensives across multiple fronts. Neighboring states remain on alert, with NATO monitoring the increased use of long‑range weapons and potential ballistic missile launches.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian coordination with the Alpha Center suggests a growing capability for precision strikes on strategic Russian infrastructure. Russian reliance on Shahed loitering munitions continues to strain Ukrainian air‑defence resources, especially in the Dnipro corridor. The reported Iskander‑M threat underscores the need for enhanced early‑warning and missile‑defence readiness in southern Ukraine.
